headline: AI Keeps Reshaping South Florida's Job Market — Here's What's Actually Happening

stories.title: Stanford Economists Drop Hard Numbers on AI's Job Impact
stories.summary: Entry-level dev hiring down 20%, call centers hit 15% drop — these aren't predictions, they're happening now. Lowkey wild that jobs needing physical presence (healthcare, trades) are holding up while high-AI exposure roles tank.
stories.takeaway: Young workers in AI-exposed fields fr need to adapt or face a brutal job market, but experienced workers using AI to upskill are thriving.

stories.title: Tech Giants' $650B AI Spending Could Actually Benefit South Florida
stories.summary: Amazon, Google, Meta dumping insane amounts into AI infrastructure. While most $$$ stays in existing tech hubs, South Florida could see indirect benefits through skilled labor demand and engineering services.
stories.takeaway: The region's legit positioning itself as a tech corridor — companies are already moving here looking for talent and cheaper ops than the coasts.

stories.title: South Florida Tech Leaders Want to Be Ready for the Next Wave
stories.summary: At Tech Hub Pulse this week, founders and investors talked space, defense, and AI reshaping the region. They're basically saying South Florida needs to prepare for rapid growth or get left behind.
stories.takeaway: Tbh, the region's serious about becoming a real tech hub — space/defense opportunities incoming with spaceports and talent recruitment.

stories.title: AI Reshaping White-Collar Work — But Also Creating New Opportunities
stories.summary: Analysis of nearly all US job postings shows automation-prone roles down 13%, while analytical and creative jobs up 20%. Companies' reskilling choices will literally decide if workers get displaced or leveled up.
stories.takeaway: The job market's not dying — it's literally transforming. Workers who adapt to AI tools early are positioning themselves for the next economy.

watchNext.title: How Young Workers Can Actually Survive the AI Job Market
watchNext.why: Entry-level positions are tanking hard — need to see concrete strategies for 20-somethings breaking in without experience.

watchNext.title: 227 AI Engineer Jobs in Miami — What Salaries Actually Look Like
watchNext.why: Lowkey important to see real demand in South Florida + what companies are paying for these roles rn.
